data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260513T174646-0882a04e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260513T174646-0882a04e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260513T174646-0882a04e/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260513T174646-0882a04e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260513T174646-0882a04e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-90b84d73">
    <title>Bound ddx work readiness hooks so blocked beads cannot hang a worker forever</title>
    <description>
In Niflheim, `ddx work --watch --profile smart` claimed bead `niflheim-edcb2b7c` and then parked at `readiness check: starting` without spawning an agent child, timing out, recording a terminal non-success outcome, or releasing/blocking the bead. The readiness check was for a live soak bead whose previous evidence already showed PgWire unavailable on `127.0.0.1:5433`.

Observed behavior on 2026-05-13:
- Worker was alive in tmux (`ddx` pane process), but no child agent process existed.
- Worker log stopped after `▶ niflheim-edcb2b7c ...` and `readiness check: starting`.
- Bead remained `in_progress`/claimed.
- Queue had many ready beads, but the worker could not move past this readiness phase.

Expected behavior: readiness hooks must be bounded. If readiness cannot complete inside a configured/default timeout, `ddx work` should write evidence explaining the failed readiness command, map the outcome to blocked/open according to the no_changes contract, unclaim the bead when appropriate, and continue draining other ready work instead of parking the whole worker.

This is separate from the underlying Niflheim PgWire availability bug; DDx should not require the target service bug to be fixed before the queue can keep moving.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `ddx work` readiness/preflight hooks run under a bounded timeout with a clear default and, if configurable, documented config/flag behavior.
2. On readiness timeout, the attempt records evidence containing the readiness command/context, timeout duration, and resulting bead outcome.
3. A timed-out readiness check does not leave the bead indefinitely claimed/in_progress.
4. In watch mode, after handling the timed-out readiness outcome, the worker can continue to other eligible ready beads.
5. Regression coverage proves a hanging readiness hook cannot block the worker indefinitely.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:work, readiness, timeout, worker, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="25b324d342dd04863feb3796b7ce0425e7a531b9" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260513T174646-0882a04e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed as `77d27abe7` with subject `fix: bound ddx work readiness timeout [ddx-90b84d73]`.

What changed:
- Added `--preclaim-timeout` to `ddx work` and `ddx try`, defaulting to `30s` and documented in the command docs. See [cli/cmd/work.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-90b84d73-20260513T174646-0882a04e/cli/cmd/work.go#L96), [cli/cmd/try.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-90b84d73-20260513T174646-0882a04e/cli/cmd/try.go#L99), [website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_work.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-90b84d73-20260513T174646-0882a04e/website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_work.md#L86), and [website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_try.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-90b84d73-20260513T174646-0882a04e/website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_try.md#L72).
- Wired the flag through execute-loop parsing/spec plumbing in [cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-90b84d73-20260513T174646-0882a04e/cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go#L61).
- Added CLI regression coverage for the new timeout flag in [cli/cmd/work_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-90b84d73-20260513T174646-0882a04e/cli/cmd/work_test.go#L67).

Verification:
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed.
- Earlier focused `go test` runs for the CLI and agent coverage also passed before commit.
